migrationPolicy.selectorKey: Exact tuple (tenantId, contractId, consistencyVariantId, actorClass where required). Missing, invalid, stale, or ambiguous registry state selects legacy.
migrationPolicy.authority: Exactly one request-path authority executes a command. Synchronous dual write, shadow command execution, automatic post-dispatch fallback, and cross-context transaction are forbidden.
migrationPolicy.readShadow: Queries may execute target shadow after legacy authority completes; only the legacy result is returned. Shadow failures never change the user response and are fully observable.
migrationPolicy.commandCanary: Commands have no executing shadow. A canary command invokes either target or legacy exactly once according to the committed selector snapshot.
migrationPolicy.fallback: Changing the selector affects subsequent requests only. Once a target command is dispatched, the same request cannot fall back to legacy because the outcome may be committed or unknown.
migrationPolicy.storeAuthority: First extraction retains the existing SQLite Workforce-owned tables. Route authority changes code path, not database ownership, and introduces no copy, relay, backfill, schema, or second writer.
migrationPolicy.legacyAvailability: Frozen legacy remains deployable, unmodified except approved migration adapters, and selectable throughout canary plus the 14-day rollback window.
migrationPolicy.cohort: Production target allowlist is explicitly empty at this gate. A change record must bind exact tenant IDs before canary; absence from the versioned allowlist always selects legacy.
migrationPolicy.routeEffect: WF-PRE-14 approves the runbook only. No registry, script, route, target suite, target cohort, authority transfer, or production change is implemented or enabled.

routeRegistryContract.storage: Future versioned Bootstrap-owned route registry; not Domain/Application and not a business table
routeRegistryContract.writeRule: Compare-and-set against registryVersion; every change is append-audited and produces before/after evidence.
routeRegistryContract.readFailure: Select legacy and alert. A registry failure can never select target.
routeRegistryContract.expiry: Expired entries select legacy automatically; target entries require explicit renewal.

cohorts.name: Synthetic rehearsal
cohorts.sizeRule: Exactly tenant_wf_rehearsal in a production-like isolated environment
cohorts.promotion: All required suites and rollback rehearsal pass; no production authority

cohorts.name: Initial production canary
cohorts.sizeRule: Exactly one explicitly approved tenant ID in the versioned route change record
cohorts.promotion: C0 evidence accepted, tenant approval recorded, preflight fresh within 30 minutes

cohorts.name: Limited expansion
cohorts.sizeRule: At most five explicitly listed tenants and no more than 10% of eligible tenants
cohorts.promotion: C1 observation/sample thresholds pass with zero critical stop signals

cohorts.name: Quarter expansion
cohorts.sizeRule: At most 25% of eligible tenants, exact IDs committed before activation

cohorts.name: Half expansion
cohorts.sizeRule: At most 50% of eligible tenants, exact IDs committed before activation

cohorts.name: Eligible completion
cohorts.sizeRule: 100% only of tenants eligible for the exact increment; legacy-hold variants remain excluded
cohorts.promotion: C4 observation/sample thresholds pass and Architecture/Operations/Security approve

routeIncrements.authority: Legacy reference source until target mode; target reads same Workforce authority after cutover
routeIncrements.rollback: Set tuple to legacy; no data reconciliation

routeIncrements.authority: Legacy read until target mode; no copied projection
routeIncrements.rollback: Set tuple to legacy; no data reconciliation

routeIncrements.authority: Legacy composed directory until target mode; provider facts remain provider-owned
routeIncrements.rollback: Set tuple to legacy; no data reconciliation

routeIncrements.authority: Legacy profile until target mode; teacher_self requires WF-APP-DELTA-02 remediation PASS
routeIncrements.rollback: Set actor-specific tuple to legacy; no data reconciliation

routeIncrements.authority: Target only for portalAccess=null; portal-enabled WF-CONS-03B remains legacy
routeIncrements.rollback: Disable future target dispatch; reconcile any committed/unknown request before legacy reissue

routeIncrements.authority: Target only for portalAccessChange=null; access-changing WF-CONS-04B remains legacy
routeIncrements.rollback: Disable future target dispatch; reconcile committed/unknown request without compensating overwrite

routeIncrements.authority: Target Workforce lifecycle only; portal access is not restored
routeIncrements.rollback: Disable future target dispatch; preserve committed status and reconcile Audit

routeIncrements.authority: Identity atomically resets credential and all sessions; Workforce supplies reference only
routeIncrements.rollback: Disable future target dispatch; never restore old password or replay unknown reset

routeIncrements.authority: Target after WF-APP-DELTA-01 Branch remediation and overlap concurrency PASS
routeIncrements.rollback: Disable future target dispatch; inspect by public query before any new create

routeIncrements.authority: Target hard-delete behavior with frozen response quirk and mandatory Audit
routeIncrements.rollback: Disable future target dispatch; no fabricated restore of a deleted interval

permanentLegacyHolds.reason: Create with portal access crosses Workforce/Identity atomicity without reservation/idempotency
Archive blocker snapshot TOCTOU remains unresolved
Archive blocker TOCTOU plus Workforce/Identity split commit

requirements: All named suites implemented and PASS at current commit
Route registry target entry count is zero before approved change
Rollback command dry-run PASS within previous 30 minutes
Command sample minimum applies only to command increments; read minimum remains 200

thresholds.promotion: Every threshold must pass. Insufficient samples never count as success; remain at the current stage or return to legacy.

operatorCommands.purpose: Read registry version, exact entries, expiry, and effective authority
Run gates, config, suite evidence, baseline, and rollback dry-run
Return subsequent requests to legacy within RTO
Inspect authoritative public facts and Audit-owned evidence without mutation
Append only a proven missing Audit intent under incident control
Prove selector fallback, authority, RPO/RTO, Audit, and reconciliation

rollbackProcedure: Freeze promotion and open an incident; record tenant, contract, variant, actor class, registry version, correlation IDs, and threshold breach.
Compare-and-set the exact route tuple to legacy within five minutes. Never broaden the target or edit an unresolved selector without version control.
For queries, verify subsequent requests use legacy and preserve frozen HTTP/privacy behavior; no data reconciliation is required.
For commands not dispatched or conclusively rolled back, retain the error evidence and allow only a new independently authorized request after rollback.
For committed, committed_unacknowledged, or unknown outcomes, quarantine the tenant/operation; do not fall back within the original request and do not replay.
Inspect authoritative Workforce/Identity state and Audit evidence through approved contracts. Append a missing Audit intent only when business state is proven and duplicate absence is conclusive.
Run tenant/operation parity and checksum evidence, confirm zero accepted write loss, then obtain Operations/Data/Security closure before removing quarantine.
Keep legacy available and retain all artifacts through the 14-day rollback window.

allowed: Append a conclusively missing Audit intent under incident approval
Issue a new provider command only after explicit independent authorization
forbidden: Same-request legacy fallback after target dispatch
reconciliationPolicy.completion: Exact state/Audit evidence, zero unexplained mismatch, incident approval, and route remains legacy until a new change record

legacyRemovalRequirements: All permanent legacy holds resolved by new governance or explicitly retained
Zero legacy fallback for eligible tuples for 30 consecutive days
observationAndRetirement.retirementProhibition: WF-PRE-14 does not retire AppService, AppRepository, legacy routes, SQLite tables, or provider behavior.

evidenceContract.redaction: No password, hash, session cookie, token, personal data, or raw provider credential

currentAdmission.moduleReadinessEffect: WF-PRE-14 plan completeness passes. Module Readiness remains Failed until WF-PRE-16, and no extraction/canary can start until command, suite, rehearsal, and exact cohort prerequisites are implemented and evidenced.

risks.issue: Post-dispatch fallback can duplicate an unknown or committed command.
risks.treatment: No same-request fallback or automatic replay; quarantine and reconcile.

risks.issue: A broad tenant/operation flag can accidentally route legacy-hold variants.
risks.treatment: Selector includes tenant, contract, variant, and actor; four legacy holds are permanently denied.

risks.issue: Audit failure after commit can be misread as rollback.
risks.treatment: Critical stop at first occurrence, committed_unacknowledged classification, provider/Audit evidence, no replay.

risks.issue: Low traffic can produce false promotion confidence.
risks.treatment: Both minimum observation time and sample count are mandatory; insufficient sample cannot pass.

risks.issue: A stale route registry can silently leave target authority enabled.
risks.treatment: CAS versioning, expiry-to-legacy, audited changes, and failure-to-legacy.

risks.issue: Shared SQLite can obscure which code path held command authority.
risks.treatment: Per-request registry version/route evidence and exactly-one-authority assertion; no dual write.

guards.rule: All ten route increments are ordered and cover only the ten target-eligible PRE-11 variants.
The four PRE-11 legacy-hold variants can never select target.
Production cohort is empty by default; every target entry requires an exact tenant tuple, CAS version, approval, change ID, and expiry.
Commands have one authority, no executing shadow, no dual write, and no same-request fallback after dispatch.
Any semantic, tenant, privacy, Audit, unknown-outcome, legacy-hold, checksum, or dual-write mismatch triggers immediate stop at count one.
Rollback RTO is five minutes, accepted-write RPO is zero, and reconciliation forbids foreign SQL/blind replay.
WF-PRE-14 implements zero commands, executes zero rehearsals/transfers, enables zero routes, and retires no legacy.

deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-16: Final Module Readiness decision and authorization boundary
deferredDecisions.WF-EXT-01+: Implement the approved module, suites, route registry, operator commands, adapters, and evidence incrementally
deferredDecisions.production-change-record: Bind exact tenant IDs, times, operators, approvals, and registry versions for each canary
